2605.05080.title: The Pinocchio Dimension: Phenomenality of Experience as the Primary Axis of LLM Psychometric Differences
2605.05080.paper_summary: We administer 45 validated psychometric questionnaires to 50 large language models (LLMs) to identify the dimensions along which LLMs differ psychometrically. Using Supervised Semantic Differential (SSD), we find that the primary axis of between-model variance separates items describing phenomenally rich experience, including embodied sensation, felt affect, inner speech, imagery, and empathy, from items describing stimulus-driven behavioral reactivity ($R^2_{adj}=.037$, $p<.0001$). To test this hypothesis at the item level, we introduce the Pinocchio score ($π_i$), the ratio of inter-model response variance under neutral prompting to that under a human-simulation prompt, as an annotation-free measure of each item's experiential demand. $π_i$ predicts condition-induced shifts in primary factor loading magnitudes ($ρ=-.215$, $p<.0001$, $n=1292$--$1310$ items), confirming that between-model divergence on experiential items is structured rather than noisy. Applying PCA to per-model EFA scores across all questionnaires reveals one dominant dimension, the Pinocchio Axis ($Π$): the degree to which a model presents itself as a locus of phenomenal experience rather than a system of behavioral responses. This axis captures 47.1% of cross-questionnaire between-model variance in primary factor scores and converges with item-level Pinocchio scores ($r=.864$). Marked within-provider divergence across closely related model variants is consistent with post-training fine-tuning as a key contributor, supporting the interpretation that $Π$ reflects a training-shaped self-representational tendency governing how a model treats experiential language as self-applicable. The dominant axis of between-model psychometric variation is therefore not a conventional personality trait but a self-representational stance toward one's own nature as an experiencer.
2605.05080.paper_summary_zh: 我們對50個大型語言模型（LLMs）施測45個經過驗證的心理測量問卷，以識別LLMs在心理測量上不同的維度。使用監督式語義差異法（SSD），我們發現模型之間變異的主要軸線將描述現象豐富經驗的項目（包括具身感知、感受情感、內心對話、意象和同理心）與描述刺激驅動的行為反應的項目分開（$R^2_{adj}=.037$，$p<.0001$）。為了在項目層面測試這一假設，我們引入了匹諾曹分數（$π_i$），即在中性提示下的模型間反應變異與在模擬人類提示下的變異之比，作為每個項目體驗需求的無標註測量。$π_i$預測條件誘導的主要因素負載量的變化（$ρ=-.215$，$p<.0001$，$n=1292$--$1310$項目），確認了在體驗項目上的模型之間的差異是結構性的而非噪聲性的。對所有問卷的每個模型的EFA分數應用主成分分析（PCA）顯示出一個主導維度，即匹諾曹軸（$Π$）：模型作為現象經驗的中心而非行為反應系統的程度。這一軸捕捉了主要因素分數中47.1%的跨問卷模型間變異，並與項目層面的匹諾曹分數收斂（$r=.864$）。在密切相關的模型變體之間顯著的提供者內部差異與後訓練微調作為關鍵貢獻者是一致的，支持了$Π$反映訓練塑造的自我表徵傾向的解釋，該傾向主導模型如何將體驗語言視為自我適用。因此，模型之間心理測量變異的主導軸線並不是傳統的個性特徵，而是對自身作為經驗者本質的自我表徵立場。
2605.05080.author: Hubert Plisiecki et.al.
2605.05080.authors: Hubert Plisiecki, Sabina Siudaj, Kacper Dudzic, Anna Sterna, Maciej Gorski, Karolina Drozdz, Marcin Moskalewicz
